title: Intense Smoky Japanese Chicken Wings Miso Ramen
description: A Whole30-friendly Japanese dish featuring smoky chicken wings and rich miso ramen, perfect for a festive meal. This umami-packed recipe is a twist on traditional ramen with a Kansai flair.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together smoked paprika, coconut aminos, and miso paste to create a marinade.
Add the chicken wings to the marinade and toss to coat, then let sit for 30 minutes.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and arrange the chicken wings in a single layer.
Bake the chicken wings for 30 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
In a large pot, combine bone broth, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let cook for 10 minutes, or until the broth has reduced slightly.
Add the cooked chicken wings to the pot and toss to coat with the broth.
Serve the chicken wings and broth over a bed of greens or cauliflower rice, garnished with green onions.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
